Steps.Message:
Sent by Simon at 20:35 - Our staff have now managed to restore power back to the Salem Road W2 area again.

They have thoroughly investigated our network equipment in the area, but the underlying network fault has not yet developed to a point for it to appear on any of their diagnostic tests. As such, they have replaced our substation equipment to restore power as quickly as possible.

I'm sorry this affected your power again today. This may happen again before we're able to pinpoint the issue and fix it, but if you'd like to talk to us about anything or if your power isn't on, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Just to let you know our staff have now arrived back on site in the Salem Road W2 area. They are going to test our equipment thoroughly to check if the underlying issue can be located before they attempt to restore your power again.

I know having power cuts this often must be causing you a lot of problems, and our staff are doing everything they can to keep the power on. Our time frame remains unchanged, and we're keeping in contact with our staff regularly to make sure you have the best information to plan ahead.

However, every time power goes off like this there is a greater possibility of the fault becoming visible.

Our staff are still on site and we are estimating power to be back on between 01:30-02:30. We'll keep you updated with all of our progress.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Will at 06:57 - We believe our engineers have been able to restore power to all 145 customers in the Leatherhead, KT22 9 and KT23 3 area by replacing some faulty network equipment. We're pleased that we have been able to restore power sooner than we anticipated.

If your power isn't back on, we'll need to hear from you. To report this, or to talk to us about anything else, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

We know that being without power can be worrying, but please rest assured that our staff work around the clock to keep the lights on. If you have any concerns or questions, give us a call any time.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
We're sorry you're without power this morning. Engineers working in the area came across some equipment connected to our underground network that has faulted.

They are carrying out some tests and checks to try and narrow down what could have caused this. Specialist engineers are also working to divert power remotely, and this can cause your lights to go on and off. 

At the moment we don't have an estimated time frame for when the power will be back on but as soon as we have this information available I will let you know. In the meantime, if you have any questions or concerns then don't hesitate to call us free on 105. Sent by Will at 06:43
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: We became aware of this power cut at 06:18. We’re sorry for any disruption this may have caused you. We didn’t know in advance there would be a power cut, but now that we know, our teams will do everything they can to get your power back quickly. Engineers in our control room have switched some customers power back on in your area. To get everyone’s power back on we may need to attend to investigate and make repairs. We’ll continue to update you until everyone’s power is back on.

Sent by Theo at 08:23 - Engineers are on their way. At this point we think the issue is a fault with equipment in your local substation in the Cobbold Road area. We didn't know this was going to happen, so time frames we provide throughout are estimated and are subject to change.

Our estimated time to have power back on is currently between 11:30 and 12:30, but after we arrive and assess the situation this may change. The reason for this is there can sometimes be a more complex underlying issue found by our engineer's tests which may take longer, and if not we may get power back on sooner.

All of our texts are written and sent personally by our team, and we'll let you know once our staff arrive.
